what_it_does: Defines window.MS_OPTIONS helper functions for deriving controls from kind variable slots, and defines a React GenerativeOptions component that renders those controls for a kind, tracks explicit overrides, resolves the instance live through window.MS_RESOLVE, and displays each binding's source.
observations: Exports helpers on window.MS_OPTIONS for control selection and field derivation.
Reads kind schemas from window.MS_KINDS and resolved bindings from window.MS_RESOLVE.instance.
Merges local explicit state over baseCtx.explicit before resolving.
Renders segmented buttons, toggles, steppers, text inputs, token inputs, swatches, media display, ref display, and color display based on slot type/options.
Displays a source badge per variable using either the binding source or unresolved.
Calls onResolve only after an explicit value is changed.
standouts: The component is schema-driven rather than hardcoded per kind.
Control selection is centralized in a small declarative helper.
Resolved value provenance is surfaced directly beside each control.
conventions: Uses globals on window for shared registries and helpers.
Uses React state for local explicit overrides.
Uses Object.assign rather than object spread.
Uses BEM-like CSS class names under genopt and comp-src.
Uses inline conditional rendering blocks for each control type.
concerns: GenerativeOptions assumes window.MS_KINDS, window.MS_RESOLVE, and Icon exist before rendering.
The component assumes inst.bindings contains an entry for every slot key.
The stepper always increments by 1 and does not read any min, max, or step metadata from the slot.
Media, ref, and free color controls are display-only here, despite being mapped as controls.
Text/token input fallback uses b.value || '', which hides falsy values such as 0 or false.
notes: This file is the schema-driven options surface that turns kind variables into editable controls and resolved provenance rows.
questions: Should unresolved or missing bindings be recoverable in the UI instead of throwing through b.value access?
Are media, ref, and non-option color slots intended to become editable in this component?
Does the slot schema include numeric constraints that the stepper should honor?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it reads kind variable schemas, chooses controls per slot, resolves the instance with explicit overrides, and shows source information; its generic reuse is implied by the data-driven structure.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make one reusable option editor work across arbitrary material or design kinds by letting schemas describe what controls should exist and by keeping resolution behavior outside the component.
novelty: The distinct local feature is the pairing of generated controls with binding source badges, so editing and provenance are shown in the same row.
contribution: This file contributes a reusable schema-to-control renderer, a small control-mapping helper, local explicit override handling, and a resolved binding provenance display that a fused system could keep as the generic options layer.
summary_for_embedding: A React widget and helper module that maps kind variable schemas from MS_KINDS into UI controls, resolves values through MS_RESOLVE, tracks explicit overrides, and displays binding sources.
intention_for_embedding: It was for providing a generic, data-driven generative options surface that can edit different kinds without bespoke per-kind UI code.

symbols.does: Maps a slot schema object to a control type string.
symbols.description: This function switches on slot.type and returns the UI control category the component should render. It treats enum and option-backed token slots as segmented controls, option-backed colors as swatches, booleans as toggles, numbers as steppers, and falls back to text.

symbols.does: Returns the variable slots for a kind with their derived control types.
symbols.description: This function looks up a kind in window.MS_KINDS, reports an error and returns an empty list for an unknown kind, and otherwise maps each variable slot to an object containing the original slot and the control selected by controlFor. It is the bridge between the kind schema and the rendered rows.

symbols.does: Updates an explicit slot value and notifies with a newly resolved instance.
symbols.description: This component-local function creates a new explicit override object with the changed key, stores it in React state, and, when onResolve is provided, recomputes the kind instance using baseCtx plus the new explicit values. It keeps local edits and external resolution callbacks in sync after user interaction.
